steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentences 1-2 describe Elara's prograde orbit (orbiting in the same direction Jupiter rotates). Sentence 3 introduces Pasiphae, a different moon with a retrograde orbit (the opposite direction). The relationship is a shift to a different location — not an example of Elara, not a restatement, not elaboration, but a move to another moon in another place.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: For example, — signals an illustration of the previous point. Pasiphae is not an example of a prograde orbit; it is the opposite. Eliminated.

B: Specifically, — signals a narrower detail about what was just said. Pasiphae is a new subject, not a specific detail about Elara. Eliminated.

C: In other words, — signals a restatement. The sentence introduces a new moon with a different type of orbit, not a paraphrase of what came before. Eliminated.

D: Elsewhere, — signals a shift to a different location. "Elsewhere, at Pasiphae" — the spaceship moves to another moon. Matches the structure of the passage. Correct.
